signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry
signals.text: Middle East escalation drives safe-haven demand — JPY COT LF net short −65,309 (crowded carry). USD/JPY at 159.35; 160.00 is the key level that has previously triggered BoJ verbal warnings; intervention risk rises on a sustained break above 160. Risk-off flow historically unwinds JPY shorts — 157.00 is the first technical level on a sustained risk-off move. Watch for a break above 160.00 to confirm intervention risk.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol + Retail
signals.text: AUD/USD COT LF net long +41,675 (bullish spec positioning) and retail 89% short (extreme retail short). HV30 at 6.8%, ATM IV (FXE chain) at 8.2% — options pricing a 1.4 vol-pt premium over realized. 25d RR at −0.21: mild put skew despite the bullish price trend — market hedging rather than chasing. Late-trend caution signal. A LF delta turning positive or retail sentiment shifting would invalidate the caution read.

signals.title: GBP/USD — CB + Technical
signals.text: BoE 3.75% vs Fed 3.75% — 0bp GBP carry neutral. GBP LF net long +28,426, AM constructive — positioning aligned with the carry read. ATM IV 9.5% near HV, 25d RR flat — options neutral. GBP/USD at 1.3503 mid-range; 1.3600 is the next resistance on a continuation.

signals.title: AUD/JPY — Carry
signals.text: RBA 4.10% vs BoJ 0.75% — +335bp AUD carry premium favors AUD; risk-off unwind risk elevated in risk-off. AUD/JPY at 114.021; carry advantage supports AUD. Watch for a shift in BoJ bias or risk-off to impact carry.
